symptoms.character: well-demarcated round smooth patch, no pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sudden painless round non-scarring patch is the hallmark presentation.
Tinea also causes patchy loss but usually with scaling and broken hairs.

symptoms.symptom_name: Smooth non-scaling non-erythematous scalp in the patch

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Non-scarring, non-inflamed skin supports alopecia areata.
Absence of scaling/erythema strongly argues against infectious tinea.
No scarring, erythema or plugging argues against discoid lupus.
Exclamation-mark hairs tapering proximally are characteristic of active alopecia areata.

symptoms.symptom_name: No systemic features (afebrile, no rash, no arthralgia, no oral ulcers)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of systemic features argues against systemic autoimmune disease.
Psychological stress is a commonly reported trigger for alopecia areata flares.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Sudden painless patch + smooth non-scaling skin + exclamation-mark hairs together confirm alopecia areata and exclude infectious/scarring mimics.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Thyroid function tests (TSH) and iron studies
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Screen for commonly associated autoimmune thyroid disease and iron deficiency as part of routine follow-up; not urgent.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Scalp scraping for fungal microscopy and culture
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Only if any scaling/broken hairs are seen — to exclude tinea. Not required here given smooth non-scaling skin.
Not indicated for a classic focal non-scarring patch; reserved for diagnostic uncertainty or scarring alopecia.
